_comment: v9 corpus manifest — C++ arm. The .template.json is committed; the operator copies this to corpus-manifest-cpp.local.json (gitignored, see the **/corpus-manifest.local.json rule) and fills in the local_clone_path for each repo. See v9-plan-2026-07-02-update.md for the corpus build process and methodology-minimum-sample-size.md for the guardrails (10k files per arm, 10 fires per DORMANT rule, 5% parse-failure ceiling). Note: C++ has 9 file extensions (cpp/cc/cxx/c++/h/hpp/hh/hxx/H) — the build script handles the OR'd find clauses correctly.

neg_repos.name: abseil
neg_repos.rationale: Abseil 2018-01 snapshot; pre-AI; Google C++ common library
neg_repos.note: Use the 201801 branch or equivalent commit. Abseil uses calver-style branches.

neg_repos.name: c-ares
neg_repos.rationale: c-ares 1.14.x (2018); pre-AI; async DNS resolver (C + C++)

pos_repos.name: llama.cpp
pos_repos.rationale: llama.cpp; on-device LLM inference in C++; post-2024-12

pos_repos.name: ggml
pos_repos.rationale: ggml; tensor library powering llama.cpp; post-2024-12

pos_repos.name: onnxruntime
pos_repos.rationale: ONNX Runtime 1.18+; cross-platform ML inference in C++; post-2024-12

pos_repos.name: sentis
pos_repos.rationale: Unity Sentis; on-device neural net inference in C# / native; post-2024-12
pos_repos.note: Sentis is a Unity package — adjust the checkout to a 2024-12+ tag once verified.

pos_repos.name: mlx
pos_repos.rationale: Apple MLX; ML framework with C++ core; post-2024-12

v8_corpus_avoidance: Verify these repos were NOT in the v7 or v8 corpus (per methodology-minimum-sample-size.md's cross-corpus contamination guard). grep -l any of these names under ~/.slopbrick-cache/v7-repos/ or v8-repos/ before running.
build_process: 2. For each repo, run: git fetch --unshallow && git checkout <ref>
3. Compute the filelist (handled by build-v9-corpus.ts --ext cpp,cc,cxx,c++,h,hpp,hh,hxx,H --arm cpp)

success_criteria.rules_measurable: ≥ 5 (the 5-7 new C++ rules, scheduled for v0.25)
success_criteria.rules_promoted: ≥ 1 (at least one C++ rule becomes USEFUL on v9)
success_criteria.parse_failure_rate: < 5% (per methodology paper; C++ lite mode kicks in above this)
